vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1205: HTML artifacts: validated, optionally-shared browser-viewable agent work products

plan.title: HTML artifacts: validated, optionally-shared browser-viewable agent work products
plan.author: agent:gpt-5.5

narratives.Problem: Panopticon agents can produce diffs and text summaries, but cannot publish visual, browser-viewable, inspectable work products with validation, provenance, stable links, or dashboard visibility.
narratives.Proposal: Add a first-class HTML artifact subsystem: validate self-contained HTML files, publish immutable snapshots with provenance into an artifact index, expose `pan artifacts` lifecycle commands, serve wrapper and raw artifact routes across separate origins, and surface workspace artifacts in the issue drawer with thumbnails and quick actions.

autoDecisions.summary: Use the configured Traefik domain instead of hardcoding the PRD's illustrative `panopticon.localhost` domain.
autoDecisions.rationale: The codebase defaults to `pan.localhost` through config and Traefik templates; deriving artifact URLs from that setting keeps installs consistent while preserving the separate wrapper/raw origin model.

autoDecisions.summary: Publish copied snapshots rather than serving directly from workspace source files.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD requires pending-change detection after edits. Stable published URLs need to serve the last validated/published bytes while `status` compares the current source hash to `lastPublishedHash`.

autoDecisions.summary: Keep the artifact index in `~/.panopticon/artifacts/index.sqlite` rather than folding it into `panopticon.db`.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD explicitly names a separate artifact index; isolating shareable artifact metadata and files also makes retention and cleanup easier to reason about.

autoDecisions.summary: Implement tunnel sharing only as a v1 CLI stub.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD marks external tunneling as Phase 2/out of scope; exposing a clear not-supported command avoids scope creep while reserving the surface.

autoDecisions.summary: Do not implement XSS deep scanning in v1.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD explicitly excludes XSS scanning because the false-positive rate is too high; security instead relies on validation, CSP, sandboxing, and origin isolation.

narrative.Action: Add shared artifact types to the contracts package for metadata, validation results, status, list entries, publish/create responses, and dashboard/API payloads. Re-export them through the package's public index so CLI, server, and frontend code consume the same shapes.

subItems.title: Contracts include artifact provenance fields from the PRD: artifactId, slug, issueId, workspaceId, agentRole, agentHarness, runId, sessionId, filePath, hashes, supersedes, timestamps, title, description, and unshared state.
Validation and status response types represent errors, warnings, size, hash, strict-mode findings, currentHash, lastPublishedHash, and pendingChanges.
Existing contract package tests/typecheck pass after the new exports are added.

narrative.Action: Create the artifact index module under `src/lib/artifacts/` with schema initialization for `~/.panopticon/artifacts/index.sqlite`, indexes for slug/workspace/issue lookup, repository methods for insert/update/list/status/unshare, and filesystem layout helpers for the published HTML snapshot store.

subItems.title: A fresh artifact index creates the PRD's artifacts table and workspace, issue, and slug indexes idempotently under `~/.panopticon/artifacts/index.sqlite` respecting `PANOPTICON_HOME` in tests.
Repository methods can create, read by slug/file path, list by workspace or issue, update published/unshared fields, and compute pendingChanges without touching dashboard route code.
Slug generation is 8 characters, URL-safe, unique under collision retries, and covered by deterministic tests.

narrative.Action: Build `src/lib/artifacts/validator.ts` to enforce file type, 1 MB size limit, secret scanning, local/relative/non-HTTPS asset rejection, and optional strict-mode hygiene checks with structured errors and warnings.

subItems.title: Validation rejects non-HTML files, directories, files larger than 1 MB, relative or root-local asset paths, `file://`, and non-HTTPS external URLs across HTML attributes, srcset, and CSS url() references.

Validation rejects AWS, GitHub, OpenAI, Anthropic, Slack, private-key, and .env-style secrets named in the PRD, with per-line `<!-- artifact-allow-secret -->` suppressions producing warnings rather than silent passes.
Strict mode catches high-entropy strings, inline event handlers without CSP, and missing image alt text while non-strict mode leaves those as allowed or warning-only according to the PRD.
Unit tests cover positive and negative fixtures for every secret scanner, forbidden/allowed asset path class, size boundary, and strict-mode rule.

narrative.Action: Add lifecycle functions for create, publish, status, list, URL resolution, and unshare that combine validation, SHA-256 hashing, published snapshot copies, metadata extraction from `<title>`, provenance capture from flags/env, and stable URL generation.

subItems.title: Create validates an HTML file, stores provenance, copies the published bytes into the artifact store, sets `lastPublishedHash`, and returns wrapper/raw URLs.
Status compares the current source hash with `lastPublishedHash` and reports pendingChanges true after edits and false after publish.
Publish revalidates edited files, updates the copied published snapshot and published hash, preserves the slug, and records updated timestamps without deleting prior metadata.
Unshare sets `unsharedAt` and leaves source files, copied published bytes, and database metadata intact.

narrative.Action: Register `pan artifacts` in the Commander CLI and implement `validate`, `status`, `url`, `open`, and `list` with human-readable and `--json` output.

subItems.title: `pan artifacts validate <file> [--strict] [--json]` returns structured validation results and exits non-zero on hard validation failures.
`pan artifacts status <file> [--json]` prints currentHash, lastPublishedHash, and pendingChanges for known artifacts and a useful not-found error for unknown files.
`pan artifacts list [--workspace <id>] [--json]` returns all artifacts or the workspace-filtered subset with provenance and published/pending/unshared status.
`pan artifacts url <file>` prints the wrapper URL and `pan artifacts open <file>` opens that URL through the platform opener without blocking tests.

narrative.Action: Implement `pan artifacts create`, `publish`, `unshare`, and the v1 tunnel-sharing stub using the artifact lifecycle library and provenance flags/env.

subItems.title: `pan artifacts create <file> --issue <id> --agent-role <role> [--json]` validates and publishes in one step, capturing issue, role, harness, run, and session provenance from flags or environment.
`pan artifacts publish <file> [--json]` revalidates and republishes edited artifacts, and reports no-op/not-pending cases clearly.
`pan artifacts unshare <file> --yes [--json]` disables the URL without deleting the file or metadata and requires explicit confirmation outside JSON/noninteractive modes.
`pan artifacts share --tunnel <file>` exists as a v1 stub that exits with a clear `tunneling not yet supported` message and does not expose anything externally.

items.title: Serve raw artifacts on isolated origin

narrative.Action: Add dashboard server routes for raw artifact HTML at `/a/<slug>` that only serve from the artifact subdomain, read published snapshot bytes, reject unshared/missing artifacts, and set the CSP and cache headers required for sandbox isolation.

subItems.title: `GET /a/<slug>` returns the copied published HTML with `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8` only when the Host matches `artifacts.<configured-domain>`.
Missing slugs return 404 and unshared artifacts return 410 Gone without deleting metadata or copied bytes.
Raw responses include CSP that allows self, inline artifact code, data URLs, and HTTPS assets while preventing access to dashboard-origin cookies and storage.
Server route code uses async filesystem APIs and does not introduce `execSync` or synchronous file reads in dashboard request paths.

narrative.Action: Add wrapper support for `/s/<slug>` on the dashboard origin with dashboard chrome, artifact metadata, action links, and a sandboxed iframe pointing at the raw artifact origin.

subItems.title: Loading `/s/<slug>` shows artifact title, issue, role, harness, creation/publish time, pending/unshared status, and Open Raw/Copy Link actions.
The wrapper embeds the raw artifact in an iframe whose `src` uses the `artifacts.<domain>` origin and whose sandbox attributes match the isolation design.
Unshared artifacts show an unavailable state on `/s/<slug>` while `/a/<slug>` returns 410.

narrative.Action: Update the Panopticon Traefik dynamic template and generation placeholders so `artifacts.<configured-domain>` routes to the dashboard API/server and is covered by the existing wildcard certificate setup.

subItems.title: Generated Traefik config includes an artifact router for `Host(`artifacts.<domain>`)` that forwards `/a/<slug>` requests to the dashboard API/server port.
The dashboard router continues to serve normal frontend/API/WebSocket traffic unchanged after the artifact subdomain is added.
Tests or snapshots verify the rendered template contains both dashboard and artifact hosts for the configured domain.

narrative.Action: Add Effect route modules for artifact metadata endpoints used by the wrapper page and workspace drawer, including workspace-filtered lists, individual metadata by slug, and unshare action support.

subItems.title: `GET /api/artifacts/:slug` returns metadata, status, wrapper/raw URLs, and pendingChanges for a published artifact without including raw HTML content.
`GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/artifacts` returns the artifacts for that workspace or issue with provenance and status fields needed by the drawer tab.
`POST /api/artifacts/:slug/unshare` disables a shared URL using existing dashboard auth/CSRF protections and preserves metadata.
Route errors use existing HTTP handler patterns and return 404/410/400 statuses for missing, unshared, or invalid requests.

narrative.Action: Add thumbnail generation and cache support for published artifacts using isolated browser rendering, storing thumbnails under the artifact store and exposing a safe thumbnail endpoint for dashboard cards.

subItems.title: Publishing or first dashboard access can create a thumbnail for an artifact using an isolated Playwright browser context pointed at the raw artifact URL.
Thumbnail cache keys include artifact slug and published hash so edits followed by republish refresh the thumbnail while unchanged artifacts reuse the cached image.
Dashboard-facing thumbnail endpoints never execute shell commands synchronously and return a graceful placeholder when thumbnail rendering fails.

narrative.Action: Add an Artifacts tab to the issue drawer with a grid of artifact cards, thumbnails, status badges, provenance labels, sort/filter controls, and quick actions for opening, copying links, and unsharing.

subItems.title: The drawer tab list includes `Artifacts`, fetches `/api/workspaces/:issueId/artifacts`, and renders empty/loading/error states consistently with existing drawer tabs.
Each artifact card shows thumbnail, title, created date, issue/workspace, role, harness, and badges for Published, Pending Changes, or Unshared.
Cards include working Open Wrapper, Copy Link, and Unshare actions; unshare updates the card without a full page reload.
Frontend tests cover rendering multiple artifacts with `Made by <role> via <harness> for <issue>` labels and pending/unshared status badges.

items.title: Verify full artifact lifecycle and sandboxing

narrative.Action: Add integration and browser verification coverage for the complete artifact lifecycle, pending-change behavior, unshare semantics, dashboard rendering, and raw/wrapper origin isolation.

subItems.title: Integration test covers validate → create → status false → edit → status true → publish → status false → unshare → raw URL returns 410.

Playwright sandbox test uses an isolated browser context, sets dashboard-origin cookies/storage, loads `/s/<slug>`, and proves iframe JavaScript at `/a/<slug>` cannot read wrapper cookies or localStorage.
Dashboard UAT or component integration test verifies the issue drawer Artifacts tab renders artifact cards with thumbnails and provenance for a seeded workspace.
Concurrent create/publish tests prove two artifacts do not collide on slug or overwrite each other's published snapshot files.
